comments.body: It would be nice to see the code

answers.body:
I finally found a solution.

By setting the condition type in Model2 to predicate and using $param within it, any condition tied to an empty cell will automatically be excluded from the generated rule. Only the conditions that $param have a value will be included during rule generation.

title: empty cells check in rule-engine decision tables
body:
I'm working on a decision table that includes some empty cells.

The auto-generated rule currently looks like this:

rule "Row 1 some name"
    enabled false
    salience -99
    no-loop true
    dialect "mvel"
when
    $p : Model1( name == "string" )
    $output : Model2( model3Code == ($a.code), model1Code == ($p.code) )
then
    $output.setSomeProperty(false);
end

The issue arises when column3 is empty—since $a is not generated in that case, the rule fails to build due to a missing reference.

What I need is a way to detect whether a cell is empty, and if it is, exclude that condition from the Model2 pattern. Essentially, I want the rule engine to ignore conditions tied to empty cells during rule generation.
